design_philosophy.core_loop: Position character in foreground via D-pad strafing, aim reticle at background enemies, dodge incoming fire through jumps/dives/rolls. Resource management of ammo upgrades and screen-clear bombs creates tactical rhythm.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual-action constraint: cannot move and shoot simultaneously. Players must choose between repositioning defensively or maintaining offensive pressure, creating risk/reward decision moments.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Space Western bounty hunter exacting revenge; control of a skilled gunslinger capable of precise shots while evading a hail of bullets in stylized duel scenarios.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately constrains shooting and movement into separate actions, forcing players to commit to either offense or defense rather than allowing fluid real-time multitasking. This creates deliberate pacing and emphasizes tactical positioning over reflexes alone. The 'Look Out!' warning system and explosion mirage effects telegraph danger, reducing pure surprise and favoring learned pattern recognition.

difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; six levels with two regular stages per level, followed by mini-boss, then final boss stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden stage select (cheat code) allows skipping to specific levels. Cooperative two-player mode available, suggesting difficulty scaling through partner support. Weapon upgrades (shotguns, machine guns) and screen-clear bombs provide resource-based difficulty mitigation.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary weapon upgrades appear after defeating certain enemies: shotguns and machine guns increase firing speed or damage. Vulcan gun (most powerful, grants invincibility) awarded when bullet-deflection gauge fills; depletes over time. Five-bomb maximum for screen-clear resource.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss-gated progression through six sequential levels. Stage select available via cheat code (Select + A×4 B×4 A B A B A B)
replayability_hooks: Cooperative multiplayer mode with shared progression
Target practice competitive scoring mode for two players
Weapon upgrade randomization (certain enemy defeats reveal items)
Score-based rewards system (precious metals: gold/silver points)

world_building_techniques: Character-driven motivation: Annie seeks revenge against Kid family for killing her family; Clint is reluctant bounty hunter ally
Visual aesthetic blending Western frontier iconography with steampunk/sci-fi robot enemies
Manga influence (Cobra) and Western film genre (RoboCop 3) create space Western tone through environmental and enemy design rather than explicit narrative exposition

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-telegraphed encounters. Boss stages follow mini-boss encounters; 'Look Out!' warning system and screen effects (mirage-like explosions) help players predict and dodge attack patterns.

faction_indicators: Kid family: antagonistic faction driving Annie's revenge narrative
danger_signals: Robot enemies in Wild West setting signal industrial/sci-fi threat
Dynamite-throwing enemies indicate escalating danger within stages
environmental_storytelling: Steampunk robots in Western frontier setting establish alternate-history tone
Boss stage transitions mark narrative climax points
Resource scarcity (5-bomb max) creates survival tension
